summary: Under Graham Potter (small sample), a direct 3-4-3 / 3-4-2-1 that builds through a box-4 then springs a mid-block counter as a 5-3-2. Two Premier League CFs is a good problem.
style: 3-4-3 / 3-4-2-1 — direct from the back, build from the middle using a box-4, front-3 freedom, mid-block into a 5-3-2 counter.

keyPlayers.name: Anthony Elanga
keyPlayers.note: Pacey dribbler, clinical on the counter (as at Forest last season)

tactics: Go direct, build through a box-4 midfield, and break from a mid-block 5-3-2. The return of Isak alongside another PL-quality striker is intriguing.
notes: Watch how Potter uses Isak's return after an injury-ravaged season — two CFs of PL quality is a good problem to have.
sources: John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
